body.title: [sprint] Generalize budget gate to per-usage-provider routing constraint
body.intention: I intend that cost gating is a general per-usage-provider budget constraint, with local and subscription providers never gated.
body.description:
**Problem:** budget_gate.py is OR-specific (reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY, fetches OR balance). Cost gating IS needed (not just us will use this) but must be general — a per-usage-provider budget, not baked-in OR logic.

**Shape:** Generalize to a per-usage-provider budget ledger; the router skips usage providers over their budget. local + subscription providers bypass the gate entirely (free at margin / sunk cost). Remove OR-specific balance logic from the dispatch path.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/budget_gate.py (generalize), budget_ledger.py, router integration.
**Design rules:** cost gate = constraint on USAGE providers only; local/sub bypass; general (per-provider budget, not OR-specific).
**Scope boundary:** IN — per-usage-provider budget ledger + router skip-when-over-budget + local/sub bypass. OUT — OR-specific balance API (removed).
**Test plan:** a usage provider marked over-budget is skipped by the router (proof); local & subscription providers are never gated; no OPENROUTER balance call in the dispatch path.
**Completion criteria:** router skips an over-budget usage provider (unit proof); local/subscription bypass the gate; `git grep` shows no OR-specific balance logic in the dispatch path.

[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
